impact: An EOL version of the Apache HTTP Server is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unxed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.
solution: Update the Apache HTTP Server version on the remote host to a still supported version.

Name: OpenSSL 1.0.2 and 1.0.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2016) - Linux
description: OpenSSL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: OpenSSL 1.0.2 and 1.0.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2016) - Linux
Version used: 2024-02-16T05:06:55Z
impact: Successful exploitation could result in Denial of Service.
solution: OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i, OpenSSL 1.0.1 users should upgrade to 1.0.1u.
insight: OpenSSL suers from the possibility of multiple vulnerabilities due to:

1) Missing message length checks which results in Out of Bounds reads up to 2 bytes beyond the allocated buer, this leads to Denial of Service. The attack works only if client authentication is enabled.

2) Calling MDC2_Update() can cause an overow if an attacker is able to supply very large amounts of input data after a previous call to EVP_EncryptUpdate() with a partial block then a length check can overow resulting in a heap corruption.
3) A malfored SHA512 TLS session ticket resulting in an Out of Bounds read which leads to service crash.
4) Unchecking the return value of BN_div_word() function causing an Out of Bounds write if it is used with an overly large BIGNUM. TLS is not aected.
5) Misusing OBJ_obj2txt() function by the function TS_OBJ_print_bio() will results in Out of Bounds reads when large OIDs are presented.
6) DTLS out-of-order messages handling which enable an attacker to cause a DoS attack through memory exhaustion.

7) A aw in the DTLS replay attack protection mechanism enabling the attacker to send records for next epochs with a very large sequence number, this causes in dropping all the subsequent legitimate packets and causing a denial of service for a specic DTLS connection.

Name: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux
description: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a man-in-the-middle attack vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux
Version used: 2024-02-08T14:36:53Z
product_detection_result: Method: Apache HTTP Server Detection Consolidation
impact: Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to redirect an application's outbound HTTP trac to an arbitrary proxy server via a crafted proxy header in an HTTP request.
solution: Update to version 2.4.24, or 2.2.32, or later.
NOTE: Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 is not vulnerable.
insight: The aw is due to 'CGI Servlet' does not protect applications from the presence of untrusted client data in the 'HTTP_PROXY' environment variable.

Name: OpenSSL OCSP Status Request extension unbounded memory growth Vulnerability - Linux
description: OpenSSL is prone to a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: OpenSSL OCSP Status Request extension unbounded memory growth Vulnerability - L.
Version used: 2024-02-15T05:05:40Z
impact: Successful exploitation could result in service crash.
solution: OpenSSL 1.1.0 users should upgrade to 1.1.0a. OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i.
insight: OpenSSL suers from the possibility of DoS attack through sending a large OCSP Status Request extensions which lead to unbounded memory growth on the server which in turn lead to denial of service.

Name: OpenSSL: Integer overow in CipherUpdate (CVE-2021-23840) - Linux
description: OpenSSL is prone to an integer overow vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: OpenSSL: Integer overflow in CipherUpdate (CVE-2021-23840) - Linux
Version used: 2021-08-30T10:29:27Z
impact: This vulnerability could cause applications to behave incorrectly or crash.
solution: Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.
insight: Calls to EVP_CipherUpdate, EVP_EncryptUpdate and EVP_DecryptUpdate may overow the output length argument in some cases where the input length is close to the maximum permissible length for an integer on the platform. In such cases the return value from the function call will be 1 (indicating success), but the output length value will be negative.

Name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check
description: Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to read secret data from process memory if the Limit directive can be set in a user's .htaccess le, or if httpd.conf has certain miscongurations, aka Optionsbleed.
detection_result: Fixed version: Apply the referenced patch or upgrade to 2.4.28
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version C.
Version used: 2025-01-21T05:37:33Z
product_detection_result: Method: Apache HTTP Server Detection Consolidation
impact: The successful exploitation allows the attacker to read chunks of the host's memory.
solution: Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.28. For Apache HTTP Server running version 2.2.34 apply the patch linked in the references.

As a workaround the usage of .htaccess should be disabled completely via the 'AllowOverride None' directive within the webservers conguration. Furthermore all <Limit> statements within the webserver conguration needs to be veried for invalid HTTP methods.
insight: Optionsbleed is a use after free error in the Apache HTTP Server that causes a corrupted Allow header to be constructed in response to HTTP OPTIONS requests. This can leak pieces of arbitrary memory from the server process that may contain secrets. The memory pieces change after multiple requests, so for a vulnerable host an arbitrary number of memory chunks can be leaked.
The bug appears if a webmaster tries to use the 'Limit' directive with an invalid HTTP method.
Example .htaccess:
<Limit abcxyz> </Limit>

Name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_http.c' Denial Of Service Vulnerability
description: Apache HTTP Server is prone to a Denial of Service vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Details: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_http.c' Denial Of Service Vulnerability
Version used: 2022-05-09T13:48:18Z
product_detection_result: Method: Apache HTTP Server Detection Consolidation
impact: Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to cause Denial of Service to the legitimate user by CPU consumption.
insight: The aw is due to error in 'stream_reqbody_cl' function in 'mod_proxy_http.c' in the mod_proxy module. When a reverse proxy is congured, it does not properly handle an amount of streamed data that exceeds the Content-Length value via crafted requests.

Name: OpenSSL: Null pointer deref in X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() (CVE-2021-23841) - Linux
description: OpenSSL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: OpenSSL: Null pointer deref in X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() (CVE-2021-23841) -.
Version used: 2021-08-30T10:29:27Z
impact: This vulnerability may subsequently result in a NULL pointer deref and a crash leading to a potential denial of service attack.
solution: Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.
insight: The OpenSSL public API function X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() attempts to create a unique hash value based on the issuer and serial number data contained within an X509 certicate.
However it fails to correctly handle any errors that may occur while parsing the issuer eld (which might occur if the issuer eld is maliciously constructed).

Name: OpenSSL: Information Disclosure Vulnerability (CVE-2016-7056) - Linux
description: OpenSSL is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: OpenSSL: Information Disclosure Vulnerability (CVE-2016-7056) - Linux
Version used: 2024-02-19T05:05:57Z
impact: Successful exploitation will allow a malicious user with local access to recover ECDSA P-256 private keys.
solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0 or 1.0.2 or later. See the references for more details.
insight: The aw exists due to an insucient setting in the 'BN_FLG_CONSTTIME' ag for nonces, which could trigger a failure to take a secure code path in the BN_mod_inverse method that results in a cache-timing condition.

Details: OpenSSL 'OOB read' Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
Version used: 2024-02-15T05:05:40Z
impact: Successfully exploiting this issue will allow attackers to bypass security restrictions and perform unauthorized actions, this may aid in launching further attacks.
solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0g-dev or 1.0.2m-dev or later.
OpenSSL 1.1.0x prior to 1.1.0g-dev, 1.0.2x prior to 1.0.2m-dev, all 1.0.1x, all 0.9.8x and all 1.0.0x versions.
insight: The aw exists as OpenSSL could do a one-byte buer overread if an X.509 certicate has a malformed IPAddressFamily extension.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
Details: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
Version used: 2023-12-15T16:10:08Z
impact: A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.9):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: A web server is running on this port
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

Name: Apache mod_ssl SSL/TLS Configuration Information Disclosure
description: The remote Apache web server is using mod_ssl and discloses configuration information through the SSL/TLS handshake.
detection_result: The remote Apache web server is using mod_ssl and discloses configuration information through the SSL/TLS handshake.
detection_method: The detection was performed by analyzing the SSL/TLS handshake response from the remote server.
impact: An attacker could obtain sensitive configuration information about the Apache server and its SSL/TLS setup, which could be used to plan further attacks.
solution: Configure Apache mod_ssl to limit the information disclosed during the SSL/TLS handshake. Review and adjust the SSLConfCmd directive settings as documented in the Apache mod_ssl documentation.
insight: The mod_ssl module for Apache may disclose configuration details during the SSL/TLS negotiation process. This information leakage can provide attackers with insights into the server's security configuration.
